_note: Two-plane decomposition for TKT-0018 / site-command-search.spec.md + .lld.md — the docs site's sitemap.json search palette, dogfooding ui-command-modal. OUTSIDE-IN nodes = the surfaces the feature touches (the generator script, the manifest, the drift gate, the mount module, the two site pages that gain anchors, the control's own filter prop). INSIDE-OUT actions = what the feature must DO. Crossed via hosts. plan:true — no source exists yet. Node ids align to site-command-search.spec.md §6 Trace and site-command-search.lld.md §2. n17 (the command-modal filter prop) is a BUILD-TEAM slice gated on ADR-0127's ratification, not this feature's own work — carried here only as a hard dependency edge into n11.

nodes.label: the site command-search feature (the whole intake)
the sitemap generation pipeline
generateSitemap(repoRoot) — walk the descriptor glob (L1) + site-manifest.json (L2/L3-stubs), emit sitemap.json {entries}, deterministic + byte-stable
nodes.accept: `npm test` (sitemap.test.ts) — a fresh generateSitemap() run's entry count matches (descriptor count + site-manifest.json row count); every entry validates the SitemapEntry shape; L1 entries carry tag, L2/L3 do not

nodes.label: descriptor description derivation — authored `description:` scalar else first-sentence-of-body fallback, emphasis-stripped, <=160 chars, ellipsis on truncation
nodes.accept: `npm test` (a pure-helper unit suite) — a descriptor WITH description: uses it verbatim; one WITHOUT derives a non-empty, <=160-char, markup-free fallback; a body with no sentence boundary within 160 chars still truncates cleanly with a trailing …

nodes.label: L1 url + name derivation — {name}-doc.html convention, Title Case name from the tag
nodes.accept: `npm test` — every L1 entry's url matches `./{tag-without-ui-prefix}-doc.html` and its name is the Title Case of that same slug (e.g. ui-swiper-paddles -> 'Swiper Paddles', url ./swiper-paddles-doc.html)

nodes.label: site-manifest.json — the hand-authored L2 + two L3-stub rows (href/label/description/level/section/index?)
nodes.accept: `npm test` (sitemap.test.ts AC1) — every row's href resolves to a real site/pages/*.html file (minus the index.html allowlist); exactly two rows carry level:L3 and each names a non-empty index field

nodes.label: generateAdrIndex() — parse .claude/docs/adr/README.md's Index table into one entry per ADR (number/title/status/summary/url#anchor)
nodes.accept: `npm test` — adr-index.json's entry count equals the README Index table's row count; every url is ./adr-index.html#{number}; a negative control (a malformed table row) fails loudly rather than silently under-counting

nodes.label: generateChangelogIndex() — parse CHANGELOG.md's `## ` headings into one entry per milestone (heading/description/url#slug), sharing ONE slug() helper with the changelog page's own anchor ids
nodes.accept: `npm test` — changelog-index.json's entry count equals CHANGELOG.md's `## ` heading count; each url's #slug exactly matches the id changelog.ts stamps on that section (same slug() import, not a re-derived one)

nodes.label: sitemap.test.ts — the drift gate (byte-identity for all three generated files + page-coverage negative control)
nodes.accept: `npm test` — committed sitemap.json/adr-index.json/changelog-index.json each byte-match a fresh generation; appending one character to any committed file fails; a synthetic page absent from both the descriptor glob and site-manifest.json is caught by the coverage check (the llms.test.ts unindexedPages precedent)

nodes.label: the mounted palette

command-palette.ts — lazy-imported per page via _page.ts's shared shell; creates ui-command-modal[hotkey=mod+k], renders the merged L1+L2+L3-stub options grouped by level, packs data-keywords=tag+description per option
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser` — every /site page: pressing mod+k opens the palette with three role=group sections in L1->L2->L3 order populated; a page where the palette is never opened loads with only the mount stub (bundle-size assertion, not the full behavior module)

nodes.label: filter=regex wiring — set the attribute on the mounted instance, conditional on the command-modal descriptor actually declaring the prop (ADR-0127 dependency)
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser` — WITH ADR-0127 shipped: a valid regex query narrows results; an invalid pattern does not throw and falls back to literal substring. WITHOUT it shipped: the attribute is simply omitted and substring-only search still works end to end (no hard failure either way)

nodes.label: select handler — location.href = detail.value; grep-checkable: no @agent-ui/router import anywhere in this module
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser` — choosing an option navigates location.href to that entry's url; a static grep of command-palette.ts finds zero '@agent-ui/router' imports

nodes.label: hash-anchor navigation — a chosen resolved L3 record navigates with #{number}/#{slug}
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser` — selecting a resolved ADR record navigates to ./adr-index.html#{number}; selecting a resolved changelog record navigates to ./changelog.html#{slug}

nodes.label: adr-index.ts / changelog.ts anchor + hash-open behavior — per-card/per-section stable id + on-load scrollIntoView (+ details.open for ADR cards)
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser` — loading ./adr-index.html#0125 scrolls to and expands that card; loading ./changelog.html#{slug} scrolls to that section; a bad/absent hash is a no-op, not an error

nodes.label: L3 lazy fetch + closed-only element replace (or deferred-to-close swap) — background fetch at module init, merge on resolve without tearing down an open dialog
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser` (a mocked/deferred fetch) — an L3 resolve while the palette is CLOSED replaces the element and the next open shows the merged set with the hotkey still armed (reconnect-rearm proof); an L3 resolve while OPEN leaves the current session at L1+L2 and defers the swap to that instance's next close event; initial page weight (npm run size) is unaffected by the fetched payload size

nodes.label: size measurement — the palette mount stub's marginal, measured + pinned if material
nodes.accept: `npm run size` run by hand — the marginal is recorded; the standing family/foundation budgets stay within their gates

nodes.label: [BUILD-TEAM, gated on ADR-0127] ui-command-modal gains filter:'substring'|'regex' + the mode-dependent #filter() test
nodes.justify: NOT this feature's own slice — a command-modal component build carried here only as n11's hard dependency; owned by the component-builder seat once ADR-0127 ratifies
nodes.accept: the command-modal component's OWN gates (component-modal.test.ts + component-reviewer GO) — not re-verified by this site feature's test suite

nodes.label: review
independent doc-reviewer GO on site-command-search.spec.md + .lld.md before build dispatch
nodes.justify: the standing design-intake DoD gate (generator != critic) — a review node, hosts no capability
nodes.accept: a fresh-context doc-reviewer pass returns GO (or the findings are applied and re-passed) against doc-authoring-standards' SPEC/LLD template + the type contract

actions.label: derive a leveled, deterministic sitemap from the descriptor set and a hand-authored guide manifest, never hand-maintained itself
give every component page a short, truthful description even when its descriptor authored none
name the single authored source for every framework-guide and package-page description
expose every ratified decision record as its own searchable entry with a stable in-page anchor
expose every changelog milestone as its own searchable entry with a stable in-page anchor
catch a page that lands without a corresponding sitemap entry, at the same standing discipline as the llms.txt gate
make the real shipped command palette reachable on every page at zero cost while closed
narrow results by a regex the user types, degrading to literal matching on an invalid pattern, without changing the control's identity beyond one opt-in prop
invoke a chosen result's own navigation, never importing a client-side router
land a chosen sub-record's hash anchor on a page that knows how to open and reveal it
grow the searchable corpus with heavy record sets fetched in the background, never blocking first paint and never disturbing an open dialog
keep the palette's own marginal weight measured and bounded

edges.why: the sitemap entry's description field is filled by the derivation helper before the entry is emitted
L1 entries need the same generator pass to attach their derived url/name
L2 + L3-stub rows are read straight out of the manifest into the same emitted entries array
the drift gate imports the generator function directly (no gate/generator drift pair)
the drift gate also byte-checks the ADR index file
the drift gate also byte-checks the changelog index file
the mount module renders options straight from the generated sitemap.json
the filter attribute is a no-op until the control ships the prop (ADR-0127 dependency)
the select handler is wired onto the same mounted instance
hash-anchor navigation targets resolved records the ADR index supplies
hash-anchor navigation targets resolved records the changelog index supplies
the anchor + hash-open behavior is what makes the navigated-to hash actually land on something
the lazy L3 merge replaces the same mounted instance the palette module owns
the merge consumes the resolved ADR index
the merge consumes the resolved changelog index
